title: Packaging: config file installed world-readable with plaintext credentials
description:
nfpm.yaml:22-26 and package/postinstall.sh

package/config.yaml installs to /etc/solar-controller/config.yaml with mode 0644 (world-readable), and postinstall.sh does not tighten permissions or ownership. The config holds MQTT/Solace passwords and RemoteWrite basic-auth/bearer tokens in plaintext (README example lines 181-219), and there is no env-var fallback or secret store. Any local user can read the credentials.

Fix: Set the config file mode to 0640 (or 0600) owned by the service user in nfpm.yaml, and have postinstall.sh chown/chmod it to the service account created for the systemd unit. Optionally add os.Getenv fallbacks for secret fields in internal/config so credentials can be supplied out-of-band.

Done when: After package install, /etc/solar-controller/config.yaml is mode 0640 or stricter and owned by the non-root service user, not world-readable.

title: Packaging: systemd service runs as root with no hardening
description:
package/solar-controller.service:4-8

The unit has no User=/Group= directive, so ExecStart runs as root, and there are no hardening directives (NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem, etc.). A metrics daemon needs only serial-port + network access; running as root means a process compromise is a root compromise. Compounded by the world-readable root-owned config.

Fix: Add a dedicated system user (e.g. solar-controller) in postinstall, set User=/Group= in the unit, grant serial access via SupplementaryGroups=dialout (or a udev rule), and add NoNewPrivileges=true, ProtectSystem=strict, ProtectHome=true, PrivateTmp=true. Ensure the binary and config are readable by that user.

Done when: The unit file specifies a non-root User=, the service starts and can read the serial port + config as that user, and NoNewPrivileges/ProtectSystem directives are present.

title: Code: HTTP API has no authentication and binds to all interfaces
description:
internal/app/app.go:111 (also epever.go:234-250)

The Gin server is started with router.Run(":port"), binding to 0.0.0.0 with no auth middleware, API key, or session anywhere. Every endpoint — including the hardware-write PATCH routes — is reachable by any host that can route to the Pi (compromised IoT device, guest, or the internet if the port is forwarded). This is the top exposure: it lets an anonymous caller change live charge-controller settings.

Fix: Add an auth gate in front of the /api routes (config-driven token or HTTP basic auth via a Gin middleware group) and make the bind address configurable (new config field, e.g. solarController.bindAddress) defaulting to 127.0.0.1 so a reverse proxy/VPN must front it explicitly. Keep /metrics and the SPA on whatever exposure the operator chooses, but require auth on all write endpoints.

Done when: A request to any PATCH /api/epever/* endpoint without valid credentials returns 401; the server binds to the configured bindAddress (default 127.0.0.1) rather than unconditionally to 0.0.0.0.

title: Test: Tautological assertions on state the code under test cannot modify
description:
internal/publishers/factory_test.go:226, internal/publishers/multi_publisher_test.go:160, internal/publishers/sns/publisher_test.go:121, internal/publishers/solace/publisher_test.go:127

Four publisher test files assert conditions no production change can fail: factory_test.go type-asserts an interface still holds a freshly constructed *NoOpPublisher (interfaces cannot change dynamic type from method calls); multi_publisher_test.go asserts the test-local slice length was not modified (MultiPublisher gets a copy of the slice header); the SNS and Solace disabled-publisher tests assert client/publisher fields that neither Publish nor Close ever assigns and which the tests themselves initialized. The assertions and comments mislead readers into believing an invariant is verified when the only real value is an implicit no-panic check.

Fix: Delete the tautological assertions in all four files; keep the Publish/Close calls as explicit no-panic smoke tests with intent stated in the name or a comment; add a package-level 'var _ publish.MessagePublisher = (*publish.NoOpPublisher)(nil)' for interface conformance.

Done when: No test in these four files asserts on a value constructed or initialized by the test itself that the code under test cannot mutate; the NoOpPublisher interface check (if kept) is a package-level var _ declaration.

title: Test: MQTT TestClose_DisabledPublisher tests wrong code path, nil-client panic
description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:327

TestClose_DisabledPublisher supplies a non-nil mock client, so it never exercises the real disabled path where client == nil. A truly disabled publisher Close() has no nil guard and would panic. TestClose_ValidPublisher is structurally identical — the two are duplicates.

Fix: Add a test TestClose_NilClient_DoesNotPanic that calls Close() on a Publisher{client: nil}. Add a nil guard to Close() in the source. Delete the duplicate test or rename it accurately.

Done when: There is a test that calls Close() on a publisher with a nil client without panicking; the source Close() has a nil guard; no two tests are structurally identical.

title: Test: MQTT TestClose_DisabledPublisher tests wrong code path, nil-client panic
description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:327

TestClose_DisabledPublisher supplies a non-nil mock client, so it never exercises the real disabled path where client == nil. A truly disabled publisher Close() has no nil guard and would panic. TestClose_ValidPublisher is structurally identical — the two are duplicates.

Fix: Add a test TestClose_NilClient_DoesNotPanic that calls Close() on a Publisher{client: nil}. Add a nil guard to Close() in the source. Delete the duplicate test or rename it accurately.

Done when: There is a test that calls Close() on a publisher with a nil client without panicking; the source Close() has a nil guard; no two tests are structurally identical.

title: Test: Integration subtests share mutable state via single SQS queue
description:
internal/sns/integration_test.go:23

All three subtests share one SQS queue and publisher. If PublishSingleMessage leaks a message, PublishMultipleMessages receives 4 instead of 3. Subtests cannot run in isolation or in parallel.

Fix: Create a fresh SQS queue (and optionally a fresh SNS topic + subscription) per subtest, scoped with t.Cleanup.

Done when: Each subtest can be run independently (e.g. go test -run TestSNSPublisherIntegration/PublishMultipleMessages) and receive exactly the messages it published, with no dependency on execution order.

title: Test: Integration subtests share mutable state via single SQS queue
description:
internal/sns/integration_test.go:23

All three subtests share one SQS queue and publisher. If PublishSingleMessage leaks a message, PublishMultipleMessages receives 4 instead of 3. Subtests cannot run in isolation or in parallel.

Fix: Create a fresh SQS queue (and optionally a fresh SNS topic + subscription) per subtest, scoped with t.Cleanup.

Done when: Each subtest can be run independently (e.g. go test -run TestSNSPublisherIntegration/PublishMultipleMessages) and receive exactly the messages it published, with no dependency on execution order.

title: Test: Race condition between background goroutine and explicit collectAndPublish
description:
internal/controllers/epever/epever_test.go:39

NewController fires go controller.collectAndPublish() immediately. The test then calls collectAndPublish() directly, racing against the goroutine. If the goroutine wins the mutex, the explicit call returns early with zero publish calls, making both subtests unreliable.

Fix: Use a test-only constructor that does not start the scheduler/background goroutine, or expose a synchronous method. The simplest approach is to construct the Collector directly and call the publish logic separately, avoiding the concurrent goroutine entirely.

Done when: go test -race -count=100 ./internal/controllers/epever/... passes consistently, and neither subtest can silently produce zero publish calls.

title: Test: Race condition between background goroutine and explicit collectAndPublish
description:
internal/controllers/epever/epever_test.go:39

NewController fires go controller.collectAndPublish() immediately. The test then calls collectAndPublish() directly, racing against the goroutine. If the goroutine wins the mutex, the explicit call returns early with zero publish calls, making both subtests unreliable.

Fix: Use a test-only constructor that does not start the scheduler/background goroutine, or expose a synchronous method. The simplest approach is to construct the Collector directly and call the publish logic separately, avoiding the concurrent goroutine entirely.

Done when: go test -race -count=100 ./internal/controllers/epever/... passes consistently, and neither subtest can silently produce zero publish calls.

title: Test: TopicPrefix tests never validate prefix resolution logic
description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:140, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:81, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:77

All three TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests use Enabled: false to avoid connection attempts, which causes the publisher to return an empty struct with topicPrefix: "". Every test case asserts pub.topicPrefix != "" which always passes. The table-driven cases give false confidence that prefix resolution logic works, but the assertions never exercise it. If the prefix selection logic were deleted, these tests would still pass.

Fix: For each publisher (MQTT, Solace, SNS), either (a) extract the prefix resolution logic into a standalone function that can be unit tested without connecting, or (b) inject a mock client so the publisher can be created in enabled mode without a real connection, then assert the resolved topicPrefix matches expected values for each table case (param wins over config, config wins over default, default is "solar").

Done when: Each of the three TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests has at least one case where the resolved topicPrefix is checked against a non-empty expected value that differs per table entry, and the test would fail if the prefix resolution logic were removed.

title: Test: TopicPrefix tests never validate prefix resolution logic
description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:140, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:81, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:77

All three TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests use Enabled: false to avoid connection attempts, which causes the publisher to return an empty struct with topicPrefix: "". Every test case asserts pub.topicPrefix != "" which always passes. The table-driven cases give false confidence that prefix resolution logic works, but the assertions never exercise it. If the prefix selection logic were deleted, these tests would still pass.

Fix: For each publisher (MQTT, Solace, SNS), either (a) extract the prefix resolution logic into a standalone function that can be unit tested without connecting, or (b) inject a mock client so the publisher can be created in enabled mode without a real connection, then assert the resolved topicPrefix matches expected values for each table case (param wins over config, config wins over default, default is "solar").

Done when: Each of the three TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests has at least one case where the resolved topicPrefix is checked against a non-empty expected value that differs per table entry, and the test would fail if the prefix resolution logic were removed.

title: Code: credentials transmitted over plaintext by default, no validation warning
description:
internal/remotewrite/configuration.go:38-53, internal/mqtt/publisher.go:11-17, internal/solace/publisher.go:15-22

MQTT/Solace configs expose no TLS options; transport security depends entirely on the URL scheme, and the documented examples use plaintext (mqtt://broker:1883, tcp://solace-broker:55555). RemoteWrite Validate() explicitly permits http and the example uses http://. When auth is configured, credentials are then sent over unencrypted channels, and nothing warns the operator.

Fix: In each publisher's config validation, log a warning (or reject) when a password/token/basic-auth is set alongside a plaintext scheme (mqtt://, tcp://, http://). Document tls:// / ssl:// / https:// as the recommended schemes when auth is used.

Done when: Configuring auth with a plaintext transport scheme produces a startup warning in each of the MQTT, Solace, and RemoteWrite publishers.

title: Code: PATCH handlers read unbounded JSON request bodies
description:
internal/controllers/epever/configurer.go:465, 607, 772, 959

PATCH handlers call c.BindJSON with no MaxBytesReader. Gin's default MaxMultipartMemory does not apply to JSON bodies, so an attacker can POST an arbitrarily large JSON body that is read into memory before parsing — a memory-exhaustion vector on a low-RAM Pi.

Fix: Wrap the request body with http.MaxBytesReader (e.g. a few KB cap appropriate for these small config payloads) before BindJSON, or apply a global body-limit middleware.

Done when: A PATCH body larger than the configured cap is rejected before full buffering, on all four config PATCH handlers.

title: Code: HTTP server sets no timeouts (slow-loris DoS)
description:
internal/app/app.go:111

router.Run uses Gin's default http.Server with ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, ReadHeaderTimeout, and IdleTimeout all unset (zero = no timeout) and no MaxHeaderBytes. A slow-loris / slow-read client can hold connections open indefinitely — a cheap DoS against a single-board device.

Fix: Construct an explicit http.Server{Handler: router, ReadHeaderTimeout, ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes} and call ListenAndServe on it instead of router.Run.

Done when: The server is started via an explicit http.Server with non-zero ReadHeaderTimeout/ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout/IdleTimeout.

title: Code: HTTP server has no TLS support
description:
internal/app/app.go:111

The server uses router.Run (plain HTTP ListenAndServe) with no RunTLS or cert configuration. All traffic, including credentialed config writes once auth is added, is cleartext on the LAN.

Fix: Add optional TLS config (cert/key paths in solarController config); when present, start via router.RunTLS. Document fronting with a TLS-terminating reverse proxy as the alternative.

Done when: With tls cert/key configured, the server serves HTTPS; without, it falls back to HTTP as today.

title: Code: PATCH endpoints lack absolute-bounds validation and silently drop/overflow fields
description:
internal/controllers/epever/configurer.go (handlers ~465, 604-675, 766-823, 956-1013)

Input validation on the hardware-write PATCH handlers is partial and bypassable. validateVoltageParameters only checks voltage *ordering* on 5 fields, with no absolute magnitude limits, so an attacker (or a mistake) can push the whole setpoint ladder to extreme values. battery-profile PATCH has zero value validation (batteryType, batteryCapacity, tempCompCoefficient written directly). Values are cast uint16(x*100), so anything >= 655.36 V silently wraps around. Per-field json.Unmarshal errors are ignored (if err == nil), so malformed fields are silently skipped rather than rejected, and TimePatch writes an unvalidated timestamp truncated to single bytes.

Fix: Add absolute min/max bounds for every writable register (charge/discharge voltages, capacity, temp-comp coefficient, RTC fields) checked before any Modbus write; reject the whole request with 400 if any field is out of range or fails to unmarshal (do not silently skip); guard the uint16(x*100) casts against overflow.

Done when: A PATCH with an out-of-range voltage, an overflowing value (>=655.36), or a malformed field returns 400 and performs no Modbus write; battery-profile and time fields are range-checked.

title: Test: /api/info verified by substring matching with colliding fixtures
description:
internal/app/app_test.go:102

TestApplication_InfoEndpoint asserts Contains(body, ...) for Version='test' and GitCommit='test123' - but 'test' is a substring of 'test123', so the Version assertion is satisfied by the GitCommit value alone, and the JSON key names (version, buildTime, gitCommit) are never checked at all. Dropping the Version field from the response entirely, or renaming any JSON key, would still pass. The test's primary contract - the info endpoint returns version metadata under the documented keys - is not actually verified.

Fix: Unmarshal the /api/info body into a VersionInfo (or map[string]string) and assert field-by-field equality against the fixture, using collision-free fixture values in getTestVersionInfo (e.g. Version 'v-ver', GitCommit 'g-commit', BuildTime 'b-time').

Done when: The test decodes the response and fails if any of the three fields or JSON key names is missing or wrong, with fixture values where no field value is a substring of another.

title: Test: Data race on receivedAuth in TestPublishWithBearerToken
description:
internal/publishers/remotewrite/publisher_test.go:351

receivedAuth is written by the httptest handler goroutine and read by the test goroutine with no mutex, channel, or atomic - unlike the sibling tests TestPublishWithMockServer and TestPublishError in the same file, which guard their shared state with a sync.Mutex. The only ordering is a fixed 100ms sleep, so go test -race can flag or spuriously fail this test with fully correct production code, eroding trust in the suite.

Fix: Guard receivedAuth with the same sync.Mutex pattern used in TestPublishWithMockServer (lock in the handler when writing, lock in the test when reading), or send the header value over a buffered channel and receive it in the test.

Done when: All reads and writes of receivedAuth (or its replacement channel) are synchronized, matching the mutex pattern of the other two mock-server tests, and the test passes under go test -race without the sleep being load-bearing.

title: Test: Tests claim scenarios never exercised (ControllerRegistration, factory RemoteWrite mislabel)
description:
internal/app/app_test.go:184, internal/publishers/factory_test.go:132

TestApplication_ControllerRegistration has an expectEndpoint flag with an assertion branch for the registered case, but both table entries set expectEndpoint: false - the positive branch is dead code, and deleting the RegisterEndpoints loop from setupRoutes would still pass. In factory_test.go the case named 'RemoteWrite disabled - returns empty RemoteWrite publisher' actually configures Enabled: true with a valid URL and asserts the enabled outcome. In both, a reader skims the name, concludes the scenario is covered, and stops looking. (Note: closed bead sctl-wfo fixed the URL path this test probes, but the registration-happens case is still never exercised.)

Fix: Add a table entry or test that injects a fake controllers.SolarController registering a known route (adding a constructor seam if needed) and assert the route responds; rename the factory case to 'RemoteWrite enabled with valid URL - returns RemoteWrite publisher' and add a genuine disabled case if that path warrants one.

Done when: At least one ControllerRegistration subtest runs with expectEndpoint=true and fails if RegisterEndpoints is no longer called in setupRoutes; no factory test case name contradicts its config.

title: Test: MQTT timeout and error tests verify nothing about those branches
description:
internal/publishers/mqtt/publisher_test.go:221, internal/publishers/mqtt/publisher_test.go:249

TestPublish_Timeout asserts only that elapsed time is under 6 seconds (vacuous - the mock returns in 100ms) and that one publish call was recorded; TestPublish_Error wires ErrNotConnected into the token but asserts the same call count as the happy path. The WaitTimeout(5*time.Second) check and the token.Error() != nil branch could both be deleted from Publish and both tests would still pass, while their names tell a reader those paths are covered.

Fix: Have MockToken record the duration passed to WaitTimeout and assert it equals 5s; capture logrus output via a test hook (or logrus.SetOutput to a buffer) and assert the timeout and error messages are emitted, or refactor Publish to return an observable result. Remove the vacuous elapsed-time assertion.

Done when: TestPublish_Timeout fails if the WaitTimeout check is removed or the 5-second value changes, and TestPublish_Error fails if the token.Error() handling branch is removed from Publish.

title: Test: Mock-recorded side effects never asserted (SetMetrics, register failures, lastStatus, SNS payloads)
description:
internal/controllers/epever/epever_test.go:119, internal/controllers/epever/collector_test.go:108, internal/publishers/sns/integration_test.go:193

MockMetricsCollector tracks SetMetricsCalls and RegisterFailures, and the SNS integration test stores each received message body into a subjects map, but no assertion inspects any of it (dead expectations). Deleting the SetMetrics call from collectAndPublish (killing the Prometheus scrape pipeline), deleting all IncrementRegisterFailure calls, or an SNS regression sending empty/cross-wired payloads would all pass. The same epever test also never verifies lastStatus caching, so /api/epever/metrics could return 204 forever.

Fix: In the epever success subtest assert len(SetMetricsCalls) == 1 with captured status values (ArrayVoltage 18.5, BatterySOC 85) and assert lastStatus/MetricsGet reflects the collected status; in the failure subtest assert zero SetMetricsCalls. In each collector failure subtest assert RegisterFailureCount and the failing address/register type; assert zero in the success subtest. In the SNS multi-message test assert JSONEq between each published payload and the received Message for its subject, and loop ReceiveMessage until 3 messages or deadline.

Done when: Every spy field written by these mocks (SetMetricsCalls, RegisterFailures, subjects map values) is read by at least one assertion, and a test fails if the lastStatus assignment is removed from collectAndPublish.

title: Test: Cover publisher enabled paths (Solace publish/close, SNS NewPublisher, MQTT constructor)
description:
internal/publishers/solace/publisher_test.go:196, internal/publishers/sns/publisher.go:44, internal/publishers/mqtt/publisher.go:47

All three publishers test only disabled/guard branches. Solace's enabled Publish (topic join, message build, 5s timeout select) and Close (Terminate/Disconnect) have zero coverage. SNS integration tests deliberately bypass the exported NewPublisher via the internal newPublisherWithConfig hook, so region wiring and the LoadDefaultConfig error path never run under test. MQTT's enabled constructor path (connect-failure error return, topicPrefix propagation) is untested. Dropping the Solace topic separator, deleting config.WithRegion in SNS, or hardcoding the MQTT topicPrefix would pass all existing tests.

Fix: Wrap the vendor clients behind small package-local interfaces so unit tests can inject fakes and assert the exact topic string, payload, and error returns (Solace: assert Terminate then Disconnect on Close). For SNS, add an integration subtest that drives the exported NewPublisher with t.Setenv pointing AWS_ENDPOINT_URL and credentials at LocalStack, then publishes and verifies delivery.

Done when: Each publisher has at least one test that fails if its enabled-path topic construction, config wiring (region/topicPrefix), or connect/verify error return is broken.

title: Test: Add coverage for epever client, configurer handlers, and PrometheusCollector (zero-coverage files)
description:
internal/controllers/epever/client.go:25, configurer_battery.go:50, configurer_charging.go:36, configurer_time.go:16, prometheus.go:144

No test references SerialModbusClient, BatteryProfileGet/Patch, ChargingParametersGet/Patch, TimeGet/Patch, or PrometheusCollector - every test substitutes mocks, so these files are not exercised directly or transitively. They contain real behavior: retry/timeout/mutex policy in client.go; userDefined battery guard, 12-register block writes with x100 scaling, and cache merging in the charging PATCH handler; a non-obvious RTC byte permutation in the time handlers; 11 near-identical field-to-gauge lines in SetMetrics. Regressions like dropping the tempCompCoefficient voltage scaling, swapping RTC day/hour bytes, or a copy-paste gauge swap would ship silently.

Fix: Add handler tests using the existing MockModbusClient asserting exact register addresses and byte payloads in WriteMultipleRegistersCalls for battery, charging (incl. userDefined guard rejection and validation-failure path), and time handlers; refactor NewPrometheusCollector to accept a prometheus.Registerer and assert gauge values via testutil.ToFloat64 with a distinct-valued ControllerStatus; inject a fake modbus.Client into SerialModbusClient to test retry-then-succeed, retry exhaustion, and context timeout aborting retries.

Done when: Each of the five files is referenced by at least one test asserting its observable behavior (written register bytes, gauge values, retry counts); grep for SerialModbusClient, BatteryProfilePatch, ChargingParametersPatch, TimePatch, and NewPrometheusCollector in *_test.go returns matches.

title: Code: Split internal/controllers/interfaces.go grab-bag
description:
internal/controllers/interfaces.go

The file holds four unrelated interfaces. SolarController belongs here, but ModbusClient is a transport concern used only by epever, and MetricsCollector has two design smells: SetMetrics(status interface{}) discards type safety, and IncrementWriteFailures is documented '(if supported)' — the interface is fatter than any one implementation.

Fix: Move ModbusClient into internal/controllers/epever (next to client.go) or a dedicated internal/modbus package. Move MetricsCollector per-controller with a concrete status type replacing the interface{} parameter, dropping methods not all implementations support. MessagePublisher moves under its own bead.

Done when: internal/controllers/interfaces.go contains only SolarController (or is removed in favor of a controllers.go); ModbusClient lives with its only consumer; no SetMetrics(interface{}) signature remains.

title: Code: Move MessagePublisher interface out of internal/controllers
description:
internal/controllers/interfaces.go:49

The MessagePublisher interface is defined in internal/controllers, so every publisher package and the factory import internal/controllers just to implement/return it — the messaging layer depends on the hardware-controller layer, which is backwards.

Fix: Define MessagePublisher (and the no-op implementation) in internal/publishers, or in a small neutral package (e.g. internal/publish) holding the interface plus payload types. Controllers import that package; publisher packages no longer import internal/controllers.

Done when: No package under internal/publishers (or the individual publisher packages) imports internal/controllers; controllers reference MessagePublisher from its new home and the build passes.

title: Code: Group publisher packages under internal/publishers/
description:
internal/mqtt, internal/solace, internal/sns, internal/file, internal/remotewrite

These five packages are all implementations of the MessagePublisher interface, but they sit as top-level siblings of internal/publishers (which only holds the factory and MultiPublisher). Nothing in the layout expresses that they form one plugin family; internal/file is also a misleading package name in isolation (sounds like file utilities, not a metrics publisher).

Fix: Move each package under internal/publishers/ (internal/publishers/mqtt, internal/publishers/solace, internal/publishers/sns, internal/publishers/file, internal/publishers/remotewrite). Update import paths in internal/publishers/factory.go and any tests.

Done when: internal/mqtt, internal/solace, internal/sns, internal/file, and internal/remotewrite no longer exist at the top of internal/; all five packages live under internal/publishers/ and the build and tests pass.

title: Test: SNS and Solace init() mutate global logrus level
description:
internal/sns/publisher_test.go:9 and internal/solace/publisher_test.go:10

Both packages use init() with logrus.SetLevel(logrus.ErrorLevel), which mutates the global logger level for any test package running in the same process. This is the same pattern that was fixed in the MQTT package (closed bead sctl-17d).

Fix: Replace the init() function in both files with a TestMain function that redirects logrus output using logrus.SetOutput(io.Discard) and restores the original output after tests complete, matching the pattern used in internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go.

Done when: Neither internal/sns/publisher_test.go nor internal/solace/publisher_test.go contains an init() function that calls logrus.SetLevel; both use TestMain with SetOutput(io.Discard) and restore.

title: Test: MQTT init() mutates global logrus level
description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:95

init() calls logrus.SetLevel(logrus.ErrorLevel) on the global logger, affecting all tests in the package. Other packages use scoped loggers. This is not undoable and creates cross-test interference.

Fix: Remove the init() function. Suppress log noise via a scoped logger per test or redirect global logger output in TestMain with proper restore.

Done when: No init() function modifies global state; log suppression is scoped to individual tests or handled in TestMain.

title: Test: MQTT init() mutates global logrus level
description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:95

init() calls logrus.SetLevel(logrus.ErrorLevel) on the global logger, affecting all tests in the package. Other packages use scoped loggers. This is not undoable and creates cross-test interference.

Fix: Remove the init() function. Suppress log noise via a scoped logger per test or redirect global logger output in TestMain with proper restore.

Done when: No init() function modifies global state; log suppression is scoped to individual tests or handled in TestMain.

title: Test: RemoteWrite basic auth header checked for non-empty only
description:
internal/remotewrite/publisher_test.go:323

The assertion is assert.NotEmpty(t, authHeader) which passes for any non-empty string. The configured credentials (testuser:testpass) are never decoded and verified.

Fix: Assert the exact expected Authorization header value: assert.Equal(t, "Basic dGVzdHVzZXI6dGVzdHBhc3M=", authHeader) or decode with base64 and compare components.

Done when: The assertion compares the full Authorization header value to the exact expected credentials.

title: Test: RemoteWrite basic auth header checked for non-empty only
description:
internal/remotewrite/publisher_test.go:323

The assertion is assert.NotEmpty(t, authHeader) which passes for any non-empty string. The configured credentials (testuser:testpass) are never decoded and verified.

Fix: Assert the exact expected Authorization header value: assert.Equal(t, "Basic dGVzdHVzZXI6dGVzdHBhc3M=", authHeader) or decode with base64 and compare components.

Done when: The assertion compares the full Authorization header value to the exact expected credentials.

title: Test: Factory tests missing SNS, File, and RemoteWrite publishers
description:
internal/publishers/factory_test.go:11

factory.go supports five publisher types but tests only cover MQTT and Solace. No case enables SNS, File, or RemoteWrite alone, and no case tests the error return path of NewPublisher.

Fix: Add table-driven cases for each missing publisher type enabled in isolation, plus one case enabling 3+ publishers to assert MultiPublisher with correct count. Add at least one case that triggers an error return.

Done when: All five publisher types have at least one enabled-alone test case, and at least one case exercises the err != nil return path.

title: Test: Factory tests missing SNS, File, and RemoteWrite publishers
description:
internal/publishers/factory_test.go:11

factory.go supports five publisher types but tests only cover MQTT and Solace. No case enables SNS, File, or RemoteWrite alone, and no case tests the error return path of NewPublisher.

Fix: Add table-driven cases for each missing publisher type enabled in isolation, plus one case enabling 3+ publishers to assert MultiPublisher with correct count. Add at least one case that triggers an error return.

Done when: All five publisher types have at least one enabled-alone test case, and at least one case exercises the err != nil return path.

title: Test: Configurer cache equality checks only 2 of ~20 fields
description:
internal/controllers/epever/configurer_cache_test.go:136

The cache-hit, cache-expiration, and cache-invalidation subtests compare configs using only BatteryType and BatteryCapacity. All voltage and temperature fields decoded from Modbus are never verified. Cache miss test has the same gap.

Fix: Use reflect.DeepEqual or compare all voltage/temperature fields to assert full contents of config1 and config2 are identical.

Done when: The assertion covers all fields of ControllerConfig, so any field corruption in the cached copy causes a test failure.

title: Test: Configurer cache equality checks only 2 of ~20 fields
description:
internal/controllers/epever/configurer_cache_test.go:136

The cache-hit, cache-expiration, and cache-invalidation subtests compare configs using only BatteryType and BatteryCapacity. All voltage and temperature fields decoded from Modbus are never verified. Cache miss test has the same gap.

Fix: Use reflect.DeepEqual or compare all voltage/temperature fields to assert full contents of config1 and config2 are identical.

Done when: The assertion covers all fields of ControllerConfig, so any field corruption in the cached copy causes a test failure.

title: Test: Collector missing assertions for ArrayPower, ChargingCurrent, ChargingPower, EnergyGeneratedDaily
description:
internal/controllers/epever/collector_test.go:66

The success subtest provides mock data for all metrics but only asserts 8 of 12. The four unasserted fields use ParseFloat32 (32-bit) parsing. A regression in that logic would be invisible.

Fix: Add ArrayPower, ChargingCurrent, ChargingPower, and EnergyGeneratedDaily to the tests table with expected decoded values (9.62 W, 4.8 A, 6.14 W, 15.5 kWh).

Done when: All 10 numeric fields of ControllerStatus that are set from Modbus data have explicit expected-value assertions.

title: Test: Collector missing assertions for ArrayPower, ChargingCurrent, ChargingPower, EnergyGeneratedDaily
description:
internal/controllers/epever/collector_test.go:66

The success subtest provides mock data for all metrics but only asserts 8 of 12. The four unasserted fields use ParseFloat32 (32-bit) parsing. A regression in that logic would be invisible.

Fix: Add ArrayPower, ChargingCurrent, ChargingPower, and EnergyGeneratedDaily to the tests table with expected decoded values (9.62 W, 4.8 A, 6.14 W, 15.5 kWh).

Done when: All 10 numeric fields of ControllerStatus that are set from Modbus data have explicit expected-value assertions.

title: Test: Collector has duplicate error subtests and three untested error paths
description:
internal/controllers/epever/collector_test.go:104

The two error subtests both inject errors at the same register (regArrayVoltage). The three subsequent individual-read error paths (regBatterySOC, regEnergyGeneratedDaily, regControllerStatus) are completely untested.

Fix: Remove the duplicate subtest. Add three distinct subtests, one for each individual register error path. Each should confirm the returned error is non-nil.

Done when: Each of the four distinct error paths has its own subtest, and no two subtests inject an error at the same register address.

title: Test: Collector has duplicate error subtests and three untested error paths
description:
internal/controllers/epever/collector_test.go:104

The two error subtests both inject errors at the same register (regArrayVoltage). The three subsequent individual-read error paths (regBatterySOC, regEnergyGeneratedDaily, regControllerStatus) are completely untested.

Fix: Remove the duplicate subtest. Add three distinct subtests, one for each individual register error path. Each should confirm the returned error is non-nil.

Done when: Each of the four distinct error paths has its own subtest, and no two subtests inject an error at the same register address.

title: Test: Epever success subtest never validates metric payload values
description:
internal/controllers/epever/epever_test.go:144

The success subtest checks that 12 publish calls happen and that metric names appear in topics, but never inspects the JSON payload. Mock data provides specific values (array voltage 18.5V, battery SOC 85%) but a bug that zeroed every value would pass.

Fix: For at least the key metrics (array-voltage, battery-soc, battery-voltage), unmarshal the payload and assert payload.Value, payload.Unit, and payload.Timestamp.

Done when: At least three metrics have their payload value, unit, and timestamp asserted with exact expected values.

title: Test: Epever success subtest never validates metric payload values
description:
internal/controllers/epever/epever_test.go:144

The success subtest checks that 12 publish calls happen and that metric names appear in topics, but never inspects the JSON payload. Mock data provides specific values (array voltage 18.5V, battery SOC 85%) but a bug that zeroed every value would pass.

Fix: For at least the key metrics (array-voltage, battery-soc, battery-voltage), unmarshal the payload and assert payload.Value, payload.Unit, and payload.Timestamp.

Done when: At least three metrics have their payload value, unit, and timestamp asserted with exact expected values.

title: Test: TopicPrefix tests vacuous across MQTT, Solace, and SNS
description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:140, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:81, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:77

All three publishers have TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests that use Enabled: false, causing NewPublisher to return an empty struct before the prefix-selection logic runs. Every case asserts topicPrefix == empty which is trivially true. The prefix resolution logic is completely untested.

Fix: Extract the prefix-resolution logic into a pure helper function and unit-test it directly, or inject a mock client factory so NewPublisher can be called with Enabled: true without opening a real connection.

Done when: At least one subtest per publisher asserts a non-empty topicPrefix that matches the expected resolved value, and removing the prefix-selection logic causes the test to fail.
close_reason: Already fixed: all three publishers have TestResolveTopicPrefix tests that directly exercise the pure helper function with non-empty values. The referenced TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests do not exist.

description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:140, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:81, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:77

All three publishers have TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests that use Enabled: false, causing NewPublisher to return an empty struct before the prefix-selection logic runs. Every case asserts topicPrefix == empty which is trivially true. The prefix resolution logic is completely untested.

Fix: Extract the prefix-resolution logic into a pure helper function and unit-test it directly, or inject a mock client factory so NewPublisher can be called with Enabled: true without opening a real connection.

Done when: At least one subtest per publisher asserts a non-empty topicPrefix that matches the expected resolved value, and removing the prefix-selection logic causes the test to fail.

title: Test: ControllerRegistration test probes wrong URL path
description:
internal/app/app_test.go:222

The test sends GET /api/solar/metrics but the Epever controller registers at /api/epever/metrics. Both enabled and disabled branches hit the SPA fallback for this wrong path, so the assertion is trivially satisfied regardless of controller state.

Fix: Change the probe URL to /api/epever/metrics.

Done when: The test sends GET /api/epever/metrics and the expectEndpoint branches correctly distinguish registered vs unregistered controllers.

title: Test: ControllerRegistration test probes wrong URL path

internal/app/app_test.go:222

The test sends GET /api/solar/metrics but the Epever controller registers at /api/epever/metrics. Both enabled and disabled branches hit the SPA fallback for this wrong path, so the assertion is trivially satisfied regardless of controller state.

Fix: Change the probe URL to /api/epever/metrics.

Done when: The test sends GET /api/epever/metrics and the expectEndpoint branches correctly distinguish registered vs unregistered controllers.

title: Test: TestPublishError only checks no-panic
description:
internal/remotewrite/publisher_test.go:390

TestPublishError configures a server returning 500 errors but has no assertions on the publisher's behavior — it only verifies no panic via time.Sleep. It doesn't check that the publisher remains functional after errors or that the server actually received the request.

Fix: Add assertions that verify (a) the mock server received at least one request, and (b) the publisher can still accept new metrics after the error (publish again and verify the server receives a second request). Use the existing mu-guarded receivedRequests pattern from TestPublishWithMockServer.

Done when: TestPublishError has at least one assertion (assert/require) that verifies the server received a request, and the test would fail if the publisher silently dropped messages instead of sending them.

title: Test: TestPublishError only checks no-panic
description:
internal/remotewrite/publisher_test.go:390

TestPublishError configures a server returning 500 errors but has no assertions on the publisher's behavior — it only verifies no panic via time.Sleep. It doesn't check that the publisher remains functional after errors or that the server actually received the request.

Fix: Add assertions that verify (a) the mock server received at least one request, and (b) the publisher can still accept new metrics after the error (publish again and verify the server receives a second request). Use the existing mu-guarded receivedRequests pattern from TestPublishWithMockServer.

Done when: TestPublishError has at least one assertion (assert/require) that verifies the server received a request, and the test would fail if the publisher silently dropped messages instead of sending them.

title: Test: ServicePropertyMap test has no meaningful output validation
description:
internal/solace/publisher_test.go:148

TestServicePropertyMap calls ServicePropertyMap() with various inputs but only asserts propMap != nil. It never verifies the map contains the expected host, VPN name, username, or password values. A malformed property map would go undetected.

Fix: Assert that the returned property map contains the correct values for each input field. Check that host, VPN name, username, and password appear in the map with the values passed to ServicePropertyMap.

Done when: TestServicePropertyMap asserts at least the host and vpnName values in the returned map match the input arguments, and the test would fail if ServicePropertyMap ignored its parameters.

title: Test: ServicePropertyMap test has no meaningful output validation
description:
internal/solace/publisher_test.go:148

TestServicePropertyMap calls ServicePropertyMap() with various inputs but only asserts propMap != nil. It never verifies the map contains the expected host, VPN name, username, or password values. A malformed property map would go undetected.

Fix: Assert that the returned property map contains the correct values for each input field. Check that host, VPN name, username, and password appear in the map with the values passed to ServicePropertyMap.

Done when: TestServicePropertyMap asserts at least the host and vpnName values in the returned map match the input arguments, and the test would fail if ServicePropertyMap ignored its parameters.

title: Test: Seven tests discard *testing.T with zero assertions
description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:196, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:126, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:137, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:122, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:132, internal/publishers/factory_test.go:143, internal/publishers/multi_publisher_test.go:147

Seven test functions use _ *testing.T and contain no assertions. They only verify "no panic" which is an extremely weak contract. For example, TestPublish_DisabledPublisher across MQTT/Solace/SNS would pass even if the no-op path silently corrupted state.

Fix: Accept *testing.T in each function and add at least one meaningful assertion. For disabled-publisher Publish tests, assert no side effects occurred (e.g., no messages recorded, no files written). For NoOpPublisher and empty MultiPublisher, assert Publish and Close return without modifying any observable state.

Done when: All seven test functions accept *testing.T (not _ *testing.T) and each contains at least one assertion via t.Error/t.Fatal/assert/require.

description:
internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:196, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:126, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:137, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:122, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:132, internal/publishers/factory_test.go:143, internal/publishers/multi_publisher_test.go:147

Seven test functions use _ *testing.T and contain no assertions. They only verify "no panic" which is an extremely weak contract. For example, TestPublish_DisabledPublisher across MQTT/Solace/SNS would pass even if the no-op path silently corrupted state.

For NoOpPublisher and empty MultiPublisher, assert Publish and Close return without modifying any observable state.

Done when: All seven test functions accept *testing.T (not _ *testing.T) and each contains at least one assertion via t.Error/t.Fatal/assert/require.

title: Test: Configurer voltage validation ignores error message content
description:
internal/controllers/epever/configurer_test.go:132

TestValidateVoltageParameters defines errorMsg strings like "charging voltage chain violated" per test case but never checks strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg). The assertion only checks err.Error() == "" (line 135), which passes for any non-empty error. If the function returned the wrong validation error, the test would still pass.

Fix: Replace the weak err.Error() == "" check with strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg) to verify each test case triggers the specific expected validation failure.

Done when: The error-path branch in TestValidateVoltageParameters uses strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg) and would fail if validateVoltageParameters returned a different error string than expected.

title: Test: Configurer voltage validation ignores error message content
description:
internal/controllers/epever/configurer_test.go:132

TestValidateVoltageParameters defines errorMsg strings like "charging voltage chain violated" per test case but never checks strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg). The assertion only checks err.Error() == "" (line 135), which passes for any non-empty error. If the function returned the wrong validation error, the test would still pass.

Fix: Replace the weak err.Error() == "" check with strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg) to verify each test case triggers the specific expected validation failure.

Done when: The error-path branch in TestValidateVoltageParameters uses strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg) and would fail if validateVoltageParameters returned a different error string than expected.

title: Code: no graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT
description:
cmd/controller/main.go:34-76

main() calls application.Run() (blocking) with no signal.Notify / server.Shutdown(ctx). On SIGTERM/SIGINT the process dies without draining in-flight work, risking a Modbus EEPROM write interrupted mid-commit. Application.Close() is only reached via defer on the error path.

Fix: Install a signal.NotifyContext(SIGINT, SIGTERM), pass it through so the HTTP server does a graceful server.Shutdown(ctx) and the controller finishes any in-flight collect/write before Close().

Done when: Sending SIGTERM lets in-flight Modbus writes complete and the HTTP server drain before the process exits.

title: Code: unauthenticated information disclosure via /metrics and /api/info
description:
internal/app/app.go:80-88

/metrics exposes the full Prometheus registry including Go runtime/process internals (Go version, goroutines, GC/memory, FDs, process start time) and /api/info leaks version + git commit — all served anonymously, useful for reconnaissance and version-specific attacks.

Fix: Once auth exists (sctl-dsx), decide exposure deliberately: put /metrics and /api/info behind the same auth gate or a separate allowlist/bind, and consider dropping the default Go collector metrics if not needed. At minimum document that these are unauthenticated.

Done when: /metrics and /api/info are either gated by auth/bind restriction or explicitly documented as intentionally public; the decision is reflected in code.

title: Code: Gin debug mode can be enabled via config file
description:
cmd/controller/main.go:42-50

gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode) is only called on the non-debug branch, so setting debug: true in the config file (not just the --debug flag) leaves Gin in debug mode — verbose route dumps and stack-trace-rich output. A Pi shipped with a debug config runs the web server in debug mode.

Fix: Always set gin.ReleaseMode for the HTTP engine regardless of the app debug/log-level setting, or decouple Gin's mode from the application debug flag so verbose HTTP debug output is opt-in separately and off by default.

Done when: Setting debug: true raises log verbosity but does not put Gin into debug mode; Gin runs in release mode unless an explicit, separate opt-in is set.

title: Packaging: Docker container runs as root
description:
Dockerfile:1-8

The runtime image (FROM debian:trixie-slim) has no USER directive, so the container runs as root. No secrets are baked in, but this is a standard container-hardening gap.

Fix: Add a non-root user in the Dockerfile and a USER directive before ENTRYPOINT; ensure the binary and any mounted config are readable by that user.

Done when: docker inspect / runtime shows the process running as a non-root UID.

title: Test: Epever decoding tested only with non-discriminating inputs
description:
internal/controllers/epever/collector_test.go:46, internal/controllers/epever/collector_test.go:99, internal/controllers/epever/configurer_cache_test.go:68

The only charging-status input is 0x0004, for which the & 0x0C mask is a no-op - dropping the mask entirely still passes. Timestamp and CollectionTime are asserted only nonzero, so switching Timestamp to UnixMilli() (corrupting every published timestamp by 3 orders of magnitude) passes. The configurer cache-miss subtest asserts 6 of 23 ControllerConfig fields, skipping exactly the nontrivial decodes: the hand-rolled time-string byte permutation, TempCompCoefficient, durations, and the int16 cast for the negative BatteryTempLowerLimit the fixture already supplies. (Note: closed bead sctl-ydr grew these assertions from 2 to 6 fields; this covers the remaining gap.)

Fix: Add a GetStatus case with a status word carrying bits outside 0x0C (e.g. 0x00D7 asserting ChargingStatus == 1, and 0x0008 asserting 2); bound status.Timestamp between time.Now().Unix() readings taken before and after the call; extend the cache-miss subtest to assert the full expected ControllerConfig (or at minimum the exact Time string for the fixture bytes and the negative signed temperature field).

Done when: Tests fail if the charging-status mask, the Unix-seconds timestamp unit, the RTC time-string byte order, or the signed int16 temperature cast is broken.

title: Test: Remote-write batch lifecycle untested (Close flush, timeout flush, buffer-clear-on-error)
description:
internal/publishers/remotewrite/publisher_test.go:213, internal/publishers/remotewrite/publisher_test.go:382

Every publish test flushes by hitting the 12-metric count threshold; the time-based flush clause (time.Since(lastPublishTime) > batchTimeout) and Close()'s contract of flushing buffered metrics are never exercised, and TestPublishError only counts requests, never contents. If Close() silently dropped buffered metrics (losing the final partial batch on shutdown), the timeout clause were deleted (a lone collection-failure metric sits unsent forever), or flushBatch retained the buffer after a failed send (duplicating timeseries on the next request), all tests would still pass.

Fix: Add a test that publishes fewer than 12 metrics, calls Close(), and asserts the mock server received exactly one WriteRequest containing those metrics. Make batchTimeout injectable or settable in tests so a short timeout can drive the time-based flush and assert it occurs. In TestPublishError, decode the snappy/protobuf body of the request after the failed send and assert it contains exactly 12 timeseries with the second batch's metric names.

Done when: Tests fail when Close() stops flushing the buffer, when the batchTimeout clause is removed from the shouldFlush condition, or when flushBatch retains the buffer across a send error.

title: Chore: Untrack .idea/workspace.xml and .DS_Store files
description:
.idea/workspace.xml, internal/controllers/.DS_Store, site/.DS_Store, .DS_Store

Per-user IDE state (.idea/workspace.xml changes on every IDE session) and macOS .DS_Store files are committed to the repo, creating noise in diffs.

Fix: Add .DS_Store and .idea/workspace.xml to .gitignore, then git rm --cached the tracked instances. Optionally ignore the whole .idea/ directory.

Done when: git ls-files shows no .DS_Store or .idea/workspace.xml entries and .gitignore covers both patterns.
close_reason: Already satisfied: git ls-files shows no .DS_Store or .idea entries on origin/main, and .gitignore covers both patterns (.idea and .DS_Store)

title: Code: Split epever/configurer.go by concern
description:
internal/controllers/epever/configurer.go

At 1,014 lines this file is 3x the size of anything else in the codebase and covers at least four concerns: battery profile, charging parameters, controller time, and caching — matching how its tests are already split.

Fix: Split within the same package into configurer_battery.go, configurer_charging.go, configurer_time.go, and configurer_cache.go (keeping shared plumbing in configurer.go). Pure file moves, no behavior change.

Done when: configurer.go is a fraction of its current size with concerns separated into the named files; go build and go test ./... pass with no functional diff.

title: Code: Rename stale mqtt naming in internal/app
description:
internal/app/app.go:21

Application.mqtt is typed controllers.MessagePublisher and may be a MultiPublisher fanning out to five backends; the field, the mqttPublisher constructor parameter, and doc comments ('It closes the MQTT publisher') predate the multi-publisher design. The NoOpPublisher comment in internal/publishers/factory.go ('when neither MQTT nor Solace is configured') is similarly stale.

Fix: Rename the field and parameter to publisher, update the Application/Close doc comments, and fix the NoOpPublisher comment to say 'when no publisher is enabled'.

Done when: grep for 'mqtt' in internal/app and internal/publishers returns no hits that actually refer to the generic publisher; comments describe the multi-publisher reality.

title: Code: Rename root testing/ directory to examples/
description:
testing/

The root testing/ directory contains local-dev configs (config-local.yaml) and the VictoriaMetrics remote-write setup, not test infrastructure. Sitting next to testdata/ (which has special meaning to the Go toolchain), the name misleads.

Fix: Rename testing/ to examples/ (or dev/). Move testing/config-local.yaml to examples/config.yaml as a sample config. Update references in CLAUDE.md, README.md, and testing/remotewrite/README.md.

Done when: No root testing/ directory exists; docs reference the new location; make targets and scripts that referenced testing/ still work.

title: Code: Consolidate test helper packages into internal/testutil
description:
internal/testing/containers, internal/controllers/testing

There are two separate test-helper trees, both using 'testing' as a package/directory name, which shadows the stdlib package in imports and makes it unclear where shared helpers belong.

Fix: Create internal/testutil/ and move both trees under it (e.g. internal/testutil/containers for the testcontainers helpers, internal/testutil for the controller mocks/helpers). Update imports.

Done when: internal/testing and internal/controllers/testing no longer exist; all shared test helpers live under internal/testutil/ and tests pass.

title: Migrate site off react-scripts (CRA) to vite to unblock eslint 9+
description: Dependabot PRs #81 (eslint 10) and #106 (eslint 9) failed: react-scripts 5 pins eslint ^8 via @typescript-eslint 5, and eslint 9+ requires flat config. Both majors are now dependabot-ignored. Migrating site/ from CRA to vite (preferred stack) unblocks eslint upgrades; re-enable via lifting the ignores afterwards.

title: Upgrade site to MUI 9 (material + icons-material + lab together)
description: Dependabot PRs #105/#107 (July 2026) failed: @mui/icons-material 9 and @mui/material 9 must be bumped together, and @mui/lab 7.0.1-beta pins material ^7 so lab needs its MUI-9-compatible release too. Both PRs closed with 'ignore this major version' — lift the ignore (or bump manually) when doing this migration. Review MUI 8 and 9 breaking changes (Grid API, theme) against site/src.

title: Test: App SPA fallback test does not verify HTML content
description:
internal/app/app_test.go:175

TestApplication_SPAFallback checks status 200 and Content-Type but does not assert the body contains HTML. A middleware returning 200 with an empty body would pass.

Fix: Add assert.Contains(t, w.Body.String(), "<html") or similar HTML marker from the embedded index.html.

Done when: The test asserts the response body contains an expected HTML fragment.

Done when: The test asserts the response body contains an expected HTML fragment.

title: Test: Missing applyDefaults coverage for DeviceID, SNS, RemoteWrite prefixes
description:
internal/config/config_test.go:1

Tests verify MQTT and Solace topic prefix defaults but miss DeviceID defaulting to controller-1, SNS default prefix, and RemoteWrite default prefix.

Fix: Add TestLoad cases: no deviceId field asserts controller-1; SNS enabled without topicPrefix asserts solar; RemoteWrite enabled without topicPrefix asserts solar.

Done when: Each default set in applyDefaults() is asserted by at least one test case.

Done when: Each default set in applyDefaults() is asserted by at least one test case.

title: Test: TestConfig_Validate only covers HTTPPort branch
description:
internal/config/config_test.go:448

All five Validate() test cases exercise only the HTTPPort validation. Other branches (Epever serial port, publish period, RemoteWrite URL/auth exclusivity) are never tested through Validate() directly.

Fix: Add table cases covering each publisher validation branch in Validate().

Done when: Every return fmt.Errorf(...) branch inside config.go:Validate() is triggered by at least one case in TestConfig_Validate.

title: Test: TestConfig_Validate only covers HTTPPort branch
description:
internal/config/config_test.go:448

All five Validate() test cases exercise only the HTTPPort validation. Other branches (Epever serial port, publish period, RemoteWrite URL/auth exclusivity) are never tested through Validate() directly.

Fix: Add table cases covering each publisher validation branch in Validate().

Done when: Every return fmt.Errorf(...) branch inside config.go:Validate() is triggered by at least one case in TestConfig_Validate.

title: Test: Parser Float32 helper may mask byte-order bugs
description:
internal/controllers/epever/parser/parser_test.go:205

createFloat32Data puts low/high words in a specific order, but test values like 1550 (fits in 16 bits, high word is zero) produce identical bytes regardless of word order. The helper itself is unverified.

Fix: Add a test case whose value has both low and high words non-zero, and use inline byte literals instead of the helper for at least that case.

Done when: At least one ParseFloat32 test case uses a value where both words are non-zero, with expected bytes written as literals.

title: Test: Parser Float32 helper may mask byte-order bugs
description:
internal/controllers/epever/parser/parser_test.go:205

createFloat32Data puts low/high words in a specific order, but test values like 1550 (fits in 16 bits, high word is zero) produce identical bytes regardless of word order. The helper itself is unverified.

title: Test: Remove redundant sleep before SQS long-poll in integration tests
description:
internal/sns/integration_test.go:104, 164, 221

TestSNSPublisherIntegration uses time.Sleep(2 * time.Second) before each SQS ReceiveMessage call. SQS ReceiveMessage already uses WaitTimeSeconds: 5 for long polling, making the preceding sleep redundant. This adds 6 seconds of unnecessary wait time to the test suite.

Fix: Remove the three time.Sleep(2 * time.Second) calls before ReceiveMessage. The WaitTimeSeconds parameter on ReceiveMessage already handles waiting for message delivery.

Done when: TestSNSPublisherIntegration contains no time.Sleep calls before ReceiveMessage, and the test still passes with WaitTimeSeconds providing the necessary wait.

title: Test: Remove redundant sleep before SQS long-poll in integration tests
description:
internal/sns/integration_test.go:104, 164, 221

TestSNSPublisherIntegration uses time.Sleep(2 * time.Second) before each SQS ReceiveMessage call. SQS ReceiveMessage already uses WaitTimeSeconds: 5 for long polling, making the preceding sleep redundant. This adds 6 seconds of unnecessary wait time to the test suite.

Done when: TestSNSPublisherIntegration contains no time.Sleep calls before ReceiveMessage, and the test still passes with WaitTimeSeconds providing the necessary wait.

title: Test: Add config validation tests for file, remotewrite, and SNS publishers
description:
internal/config/config_test.go:8

TestLoad and TestConfig_Validate thoroughly test MQTT, Solace, and Epever configuration but have no test cases for file publisher (enabled with empty filename), remotewrite (missing URL, mutually exclusive auth), or SNS (missing region/topicArn) validation.

Fix: Add test cases to TestLoad for: (1) file publisher enabled with empty filename, (2) remotewrite enabled with missing URL, (3) remotewrite with both basicAuth and bearerToken set, (4) SNS enabled with missing region, (5) SNS enabled with missing topicArn. Each should assert the expected validation error.

Done when: TestLoad has at least one error-path test case for each of the three publishers (file, remotewrite, SNS), and each asserts a specific error message substring.

title: Test: Add config validation tests for file, remotewrite, and SNS publishers
description:
internal/config/config_test.go:8

TestLoad and TestConfig_Validate thoroughly test MQTT, Solace, and Epever configuration but have no test cases for file publisher (enabled with empty filename), remotewrite (missing URL, mutually exclusive auth), or SNS (missing region/topicArn) validation.

Fix: Add test cases to TestLoad for: (1) file publisher enabled with empty filename, (2) remotewrite enabled with missing URL, (3) remotewrite with both basicAuth and bearerToken set, (4) SNS enabled with missing region, (5) SNS enabled with missing topicArn. Each should assert the expected validation error.

Done when: TestLoad has at least one error-path test case for each of the three publishers (file, remotewrite, SNS), and each asserts a specific error message substring.

title: Code: Drop redundant topicPrefix constructor parameter from publishers
description:
internal/publishers/factory.go:25

The factory calls mqtt.NewPublisher(&cfg.Mqtt, cfg.Mqtt.TopicPrefix) — passing a field of the config alongside the config itself. The same pattern repeats for Solace, SNS, and RemoteWrite.

Fix: Remove the topicPrefix parameter from each publisher constructor and have each read TopicPrefix from its own config struct (RemoteWrite keeps deviceId as it comes from the parent config).

Done when: No publisher constructor takes a topicPrefix parameter that duplicates a field of the config struct it already receives; factory call sites simplified and tests updated.
